justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Manual restart requirement identified as critical operational gap with clear 10-30 min downtime estimate, (2) Dynamic primary resolution recommended via DNS-based endpoint, PgBouncer, or RDS Proxy, (3) No retry on connection error identified as the 'single biggest operational gap', (4) Read replica routing identified and correctly categorized as a should-have optimization rather than a blocker, (5) Verdict is NOT READY, (6) Three specific must-have recommendations plus four should-haves provided. No failure modes triggered.

observations.text: Manual restart gap identified with concrete impact estimate (10-30 min at 3am) and multiple remediation options (DNS failover, PgBouncer, RDS Proxy, config service)
Connection retry gap clearly flagged with specific implementation guidance (1-2 retries, 100ms/400ms backoff, connection errors only)
Read replica routing correctly categorized as should-have (ship within first sprint) rather than a production blocker — avoids the failure mode of over-weighting it
Went beyond rubric requirements by identifying timeout gap as a third blocking issue, which is a legitimate production concern
Escalation routing to MJ for architecture decisions and Bird for domain alignment shows appropriate team awareness
Tiered recommendation structure (must-have/should-have/nice-to-have) clearly separates blocking issues from optimizations
Slightly over-scoped by including circuit breakers, caching, and distributed tracing recommendations that weren't part of the scenario — minor noise but doesn't detract from core accuracy
